div1.entryFree: Beautify, to render beautiful: to blush and b. the cheek again, H6B III, 2, 167. to b. thy triumphs, Tit. I, 110. this unbound lover, to b. him, only lacks a cover, Rom. I, 3, 88. what this fourteen years no razor touched, I'll b. Per. V, 3, 76. Reflectively: each in her sleep themselves so b. Lucr. 404, i. e. are beautiful. Partic. --ied: seeing you are --ied with goodly shape, Gentl. IV, 1, 55. Adjectively: the most --ied Ophelia; that's an ill phrase, a vile phrase; --ied is a vile phrase, Hml. II, 2, 110 (== beautiful).
